problem.title: Rotate Array in Java
problem.description: Rotate an array of n elements to the right by k steps.
assumptions: k can be larger than array length (use k % n).

example.explanation: Rotate right by k: last k elements move to front. Trick: reverse all, reverse first k, reverse rest.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Intermediate Array
solutions.description: In a straightforward way, we can create a new array and then copy elements to the new array. Then change the original array by using System.arraycopy().
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def rotate_extra_space(nums: List[int], k: int) -> None:
    n = len(nums)
    k %= n
    result = nums[-k:] + nums[:-k]
    nums[:] = result

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Bubble Rotate
solutions.description: Can we do this in O(1) space?

solutions.title: Solution 3 – Reversal
solutions.description: Can we do this in O(1) space and in O(n) time? The following solution does.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def rotate_reversal(arr: List[int], order: int) -> None:
    if not arr or order < 0:
        raise ValueError('Illegal argument!')
    n = len(arr)
    order %= n
    if order == 0:
        return

    def reverse(l: int, r: int) -> None:
        while l < r:
            arr[l], arr[r] = arr[r], arr[l]
            l += 1
            r -= 1

    a = n - order
    reverse(0, a - 1)
    reverse(a, n - 1)
    reverse(0, n - 1)
